description:
## Finding

`SigningBackend::algorithm()` returns three different `SecretError` variants for the same semantic condition — "this backend cannot answer right now or the call doesn't make sense":

- **secretx-tpm2** (lib.rs:480): `SecretError::InvalidUri` — when called on an NV-mode URI
- **secretx-pkcs11** (lib.rs:734): `SecretError::Backend` — when the algorithm cache is cold
- **secretx-wolfhsm** (lib.rs:732): `SecretError::Unavailable` — signing stub not yet implemented

The core trait doc (lib.rs:1414–1424) says HSM backends "may require a prior call to sign()" and should "return an error rather than blocking on HSM I/O" but does not specify which variant to use.

## Impact

A caller matching on the `algorithm()` error to decide retry strategy gets wrong answers:
- `InvalidUri` (tpm2) signals "your URI is malformed" — callers will not retry, but the real cause is a logic bug (called algorithm() on a storage URI that was correctly parsed).
- `Backend` (pkcs11) signals "permanent failure" — callers will not retry, which is correct for cold-cache but misleading because calling `sign()` first fixes it.
- `Unavailable` (wolfhsm) signals "retry later" — incorrect because no amount of retrying will make the stub work.

A new sibling crate author has no guidance on which to pick.

## Locations

- `/home/mark/PROJECT/crate-secretx/secretx-tpm2/src/lib.rs` line 477–485
- `/home/mark/PROJECT/crate-secretx/secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs` line 730–740
- `/home/mark/PROJECT/crate-secretx/secretx-wolfhsm/src/lib.rs` line 731–733
- `/home/mark/PROJECT/crate-secretx/secretx-core/src/lib.rs` line 1414–1424 (trait doc)
title: algorithm() returns different error variants for 'not applicable' across signing backends
